questions.question: Explain the virtual DOM. How does React use it for rendering?
questions.answer: Virtual DOM is a JavaScript representation of the actual DOM. React creates a virtual tree, compares it with previous version (diffing), then calculates minimum changes needed (reconciliation), and batches updates to actual DOM. This minimizes expensive DOM operations.
questions.explanation: DOM manipulation is slow; Virtual DOM reduces direct DOM manipulation. React uses a diffing algorithm to find differences and minimizes re-renders. Components re-render when state or props change.

questions.question: What is the difference between useEffect and useLayoutEffect?
questions.answer: useEffect runs asynchronously after the browser paints - use for data fetching, subscriptions. useLayoutEffect runs synchronously after DOM mutations but before paint - use for DOM measurements, preventing visual flicker. Prefer useEffect unless you need to block rendering.
questions.explanation: useLayoutEffect fires before the browser repaints, allowing you to make DOM changes without showing intermediate states. Use sparingly as it blocks the browser from painting.

questions.question: Explain CSS box model. What is the difference between content-box and border-box?
questions.answer: Box model: content + padding + border + margin. content-box: width/height only includes content (default). border-box: width/height includes content + padding + border. Use border-box for predictable sizing.
questions.explanation: box-sizing: border-box makes calculations easier - you set total size and padding/border are included. Modern CSS resets often set * { box-sizing: border-box }.

questions.question: What is closure in JavaScript? Provide an example.
questions.answer: A closure is a function that remembers its lexical scope even when executed outside that scope. Example: inner function accessing outer function variables. Used for data privacy, function factories, maintaining state.
questions.explanation: Closures are created every time a function is defined. They "close over" variables from their surrounding scope. Essential for callbacks, event handlers, and module patterns.

questions.question: How does event bubbling and capturing work in the DOM?
questions.answer: Event propagation has 3 phases: capturing (root to target), at target, and bubbling (target to root). Events bubble by default - handlers on parent elements fire after child handlers. Use event.stopPropagation() to stop bubbling. Use capture phase with addEventListener third parameter.
questions.explanation: Understanding event flow is crucial for event delegation - attaching one handler to parent instead of many to children. Useful for dynamic content and performance optimization.

questions.question: Explain the difference between CSS Grid and Flexbox.
questions.answer: Flexbox is one-dimensional - layouts in a single row or column. Grid is two-dimensional - rows and columns simultaneously. Use Flexbox for component layouts (nav bars, cards), Grid for page layouts (overall structure).
questions.explanation: Flexbox: justify-content, align-items for alignment along axis. Grid: template-rows, template-columns for explicit 2D structure. Both work together well.

questions.question: What is the Virtual DOM in React and how does it improve performance?
questions.answer: The Virtual DOM is a lightweight in-memory representation of the real DOM. React diffs the virtual DOM trees (reconciliation) to find minimal changes, then batches DOM updates.
questions.explanation: Direct DOM manipulation is expensive. React batches updates using the virtual DOM diffing algorithm (Fiber in React 16+), minimizing reflows and repaints for better performance.

questions.question: Explain the CSS Box Model.
questions.answer: The CSS Box Model consists of: content (actual content), padding (space inside border), border (surrounds padding), margin (space outside border). box-sizing: border-box makes width include padding+border.
questions.explanation: Understanding the box model is crucial for precise layouts. By default (content-box), width/height only apply to content area. border-box is preferred in modern CSS for predictable sizing.

questions.question: What are React Hooks? Name the most common ones.
questions.answer: Hooks let functional components use state and lifecycle features. Common hooks: useState (local state), useEffect (side effects), useContext (context API), useRef (mutable refs), useMemo/useCallback (memoization).
questions.explanation: Hooks were introduced in React 16.8 to replace class components. They follow two rules: only call at the top level, only call from React functions.
